differences.title: 1. Performance
differences.leftDesc: Python's interpretation leads to slower execution speeds for CPU-bound tasks.
differences.rightDesc: Go's compilation to machine code results in significantly faster performance.

differences.title: 2. Concurrency
differences.leftDesc: Global Interpreter Lock (GIL) hinders true multi-core parallelism in CPython.
differences.rightDesc: Built-in lightweight goroutines enable massive concurrency with ease.

differences.title: 3. Syntax
differences.leftDesc: Emphasizes readability with significant whitespace, often more lines of code.
differences.rightDesc: Minimalistic syntax, strong typing, fewer lines, stricter formatting.

differences.title: 4. Ecosystem
differences.leftDesc: Extensive third-party libraries for data science, web dev, AI, and more.
differences.rightDesc: Strong standard library, ideal for networking and systems programming.

differences.title: 5. Typing
differences.leftDesc: Types are checked at runtime, offering flexibility but potential errors.
differences.rightDesc: Types are checked at compile time, catching errors early.

differences.title: 6. Use Cases
differences.leftDesc: Dominant in data science, machine learning, web applications, scripting.
differences.rightDesc: Excellent for backend services, CLIs, network tools, concurrent apps.

differences.title: 7. Learning Curve
differences.leftDesc: Beginner-friendly syntax and vast community support make it easy to start.
differences.rightDesc: Requires understanding of pointers and concurrency concepts for advanced use.
